refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as design drivers. The constraint linkage to safety plan compliance is made explicit to ensure safety criticality (high) is not deferred.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week); log task cycle times, material moved (m³ or ton), and safety incidents; compare against productivity_target and safety plan compliance checklist.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and material throughput consistent with productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies in real-time, preventing unsafe task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide telemetry and task status visibility to Site Manager and Safety Manager for oversight and intervention.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with on-site operator cabin and optional tele-operated control via low-latency network link; measure productivity via onboard task logging and post-shift reporting.

DP-B: Deploy modular vehicle platform with dual control modes (manual on-site, autonomous/tele-op remote); implement real-time safety interlock system tied to worksite geofence and policy rules; stream telemetry to cloud-based productivity analytics.
DP-C: Provide vehicle with baseline earthmoving capability; rely on operator skill and worksite supervision for productivity and safety; measure via manual time-study and incident logs.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Deploy modular vehicle platform with dual control modes (manual on-site, autonomous/tele-op remote); implement real-time safety interlock system tied to worksite geofence and policy rules; stream telemetry to cloud-based productivity analytics.

potential_sources: Vehicle mechanical design (hydraulics, boom kinematics) is decoupled from control mode selection (on-site vs. tele-op); both modes share same actuation.
Safety interlock logic (DP-B) is independent of productivity measurement (DP-B); they share telemetry stream but do not functionally couple.
Telemetry streaming and productivity analytics are loosely coupled via Network Link interface; analytics can be upgraded without vehicle redesign.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits low coupling because it separates concerns: vehicle capability, control mode, safety enforcement, and measurement are logically distinct. Dual control modes add complexity but do not create functional coupling if interfaces are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Low-latency network link (Telemetry, Tasking) is available and reliable at worksite; risk: network outage degrades tele-op productivity.
Assumption: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formally defined and can be encoded as geofence/rule constraints; risk: ambiguous or dynamic policies may not be fully captured.
Assumption: Operator (on-site or remote) is trained and competent; risk: inadequate training leads to suboptimal productivity or safety violations.
Assumption: Vehicle mechanical design can support dual control modes without significant cost/weight penalty; risk: added sensors/actuators for tele-op reduce payload or increase maintenance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify network latency requirement (e.g., <200 ms round-trip for tele-op safety-critical commands) and define fallback mode if network degrades. Establish formal process for encoding worksite safety policies into interlock rules. Specify operator training and certification requirements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Responsible for end-to-end productivity delivery, safety compliance, and operator interface (on-site and remote). Owns integration of telemetry, tasking, and safety alert flows across subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle and Safety Services emit telemetry (task status, cycle time, material moved, safety state, geolocation) to Site Manager and Safety Manager dashboards and analytics backend.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or on-site operator issues task commands (dig, load, transport, dump) to Vehicle via Tasking interface; tele-operator issues remote commands via Teleoperation subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services emits SafetyAlert (geofence violation, policy breach, emergency stop) to operator and Site Manager for immediate awareness and intervention.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional network connectivity for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert; supports tele-op control and cloud-based analytics.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI). All sub-functions (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their integration are owned by NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross boundary.

Vehicle owns earthmoving capability and on-site operator control, but does not own tele-op control (Teleoperation subsystem) or safety enforcement (Safety Services). Productivity measurement requires integration with Teleoperation and Safety Services; high diffusion if assigned here.

Teleoperation owns remote control mode and operator interface, but does not own Vehicle mechanics or safety enforcement. Productivity depends on Vehicle capability and Safety Services constraints; high diffusion if assigned here.

Safety Services owns safety interlock and policy enforcement, but does not own Vehicle capability or control modes. Productivity is not a safety function; assigning here would create high diffusion and misalignment with safety criticality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI) is a system-level acceptance criterion that requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. No single subsystem can independently deliver productivity; the criterion must be owned at the NGHE Solution level to ensure holistic design and verification.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; detailed productivity design (e.g., cycle time optimization, material throughput algorithms) will be decomposed to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Ensure decomposition maintains traceability to CR-NGHE-001.

Risk: Productivity measurement and verification require data from Vehicle (task logs), Teleoperation (operator commands), and Safety Services (constraint violations). Ensure telemetry integration is complete and unambiguous.

Risk: Safety criticality (high) may conflict with productivity optimization (e.g., safety constraints reduce cycle time). Ensure safety takes precedence in design trade-offs; document any productivity concessions due to safety.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear ownership of productivity measurement and reporting within NGHE Solution (e.g., dedicated analytics module or integration responsibility). Define interfaces between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to ensure telemetry and tasking flows are non-ambiguous. Create traceability matrix linking CR-NGHE-001 to sub-FRs and DPs.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 (with sub-FRs) clearly maps to DP-B (dual control modes, safety interlock, telemetry analytics). DP-B addresses all aspects of the refined FR: Vehicle capability, tele-op control, safety enforcement, and measurement. No functional gaps or misalignments detected.

diffusion_risks: DP-B requires integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary to own this integration. Risk: if integration responsibility is unclear, diffusion of telemetry and tasking flows across subsystems may occur. Mitigation: define explicit integration interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and assign ownership of each interface to a specific subsystem or integration team.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution (IM) has high containment score (95) for CR-NGHE-001. All external interfaces are well-defined and cross the NGHE Solution boundary to external stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, analytics backend). No internal diffusion detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-001 is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h) and safety compliance over agreed period. Telemetry from NGHE Solution provides data for verification. Feasibility is high if telemetry is complete and reliable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 to 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution integration respectively. Traceability is established.

recommended_revisions: Define network latency and reliability requirements for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert interfaces to ensure tele-op productivity is not degraded.
Establish formal process for encoding worksite safety policies into Safety Services interlock rules; ensure policies are captured completely and unambiguously.
Create detailed telemetry specification (data fields, sampling rates, accuracy) to support productivity measurement and analytics.
Define fallback mode if Network Link degrades; ensure Vehicle can operate safely in on-site mode without tele-op or cloud analytics.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that has been successfully refined into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 to 001.4) with clear allocation to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution integration. DP-B (dual control modes, safety interlock, telemetry analytics) is well-aligned with the refined FR and exhibits low coupling. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary with high containment score (95). All gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. Proceed to next decomposition level for each sub-FR to detail Vehicle capability, Teleoperation control logic, and Safety Services interlock rules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time and material throughput consistent with productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation subsystem shall enable remote operator to command and monitor Vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies in real-time, preventing unsafe task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide telemetry and task status visibility to Site Manager and Safety Manager for oversight and intervention.

assumptions: Low-latency network link (Telemetry, Tasking) is available and reliable at worksite.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formally defined and can be encoded as geofence/rule constraints.
Operator (on-site or remote) is trained and competent.
Vehicle mechanical design can support dual control modes without significant cost/weight penalty.
Productivity_target is defined by customer and is achievable with representative equipment and operators.
Representative operations trial is feasible and representative of actual worksite conditions.
open_risks: Network outage degrades tele-op productivity; fallback mode must be defined.
Ambiguous or dynamic worksite safety policies may not be fully captured in interlock rules.
Inadequate operator training leads to suboptimal productivity or safety violations.
Added sensors/actuators for tele-op reduce payload or increase maintenance burden.
Telemetry data quality or completeness may be insufficient for accurate productivity measurement.
Safety criticality (high) may conflict with productivity optimization; trade-offs must be documented.
evidence_needed: Formal definition of productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies (geofence, speed limits, load limits, etc.).
Network latency and reliability requirements for tele-op safety-critical commands.
Detailed telemetry specification (data fields, sampling rates, accuracy).
Vehicle mechanical design trade-off analysis (dual control modes vs. cost/weight/maintenance).
Fallback mode specification if Network Link degrades.
Representative operations trial plan and success criteria.
